library.description: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3: النوافذ، الإدخال، الأحداث، الصوت، والرسم الأساسي.

kind.description: أنواع مجردة أو صريحة تحمل معنى دلالياً وتظهر في التواقيع والبنى.

identity.name: SDL_AudioPostmixCallback
identity.titleArabic:
A callback that fires when data is about to be fed to an audio
device

summary.actualTranslationArabic:
A callback that fires when data is about to be fed to an audio
device
summary.actualMeaningArabic: يمثل هذا النوع نوع SDL الصوت Postmix Callback، أي القيمة أو المقبض أو المؤشر الذي تتعامل معه بقية الواجهة في هذا السياق.
summary.purposeArabic: يستخدم هذا النوع عندما تحتاج القيمة أو المقبض أو المؤشر الذي يمثله داخل بقية الواجهة.
summary.whyUseArabic: القيمة الحقيقية هنا أنه يمثل نوع القيمة التي تتداولها بقية الواجهة بين الحقول والدوال.
summary.carriedValueArabic: يحمل هذا النوع القيمة الفعلية أو المقبض أو المؤشر الذي تمرره بقية الدوال والحقول في هذا المسار.
summary.sourceDescription:
A callback that fires when data is about to be fed to an audio
device.

signature.raw: typedef void (SDLCALL *SDL_AudioPostmixCallback)(void *userdata, const SDL_AudioSpec *spec, float *buffer, int buflen);

remarks:
This is useful for accessing the final mix, perhaps for writing a
visualizer or applying a final effect to the audio data before
playback.

This callback should run as quickly as possible and not block for any
significant time, as this callback delays submission of data to the
audio device, which can cause audio playback problems.

The postmix callback must be able to handle any audio data
format specified in spec , which can change between
callbacks if the audio device changed. However, this only covers
frequency and channel count; data is always provided here in SDL_AUDIO_F32 format.

The postmix callback runs after logical device gain and
audiostream gain have been applied, which is to say you can make the
output data louder at this point than the gain settings would
suggest.
details.threadSafety:
This will run from a background thread owned by SDL. The application
is responsible for locking resources the callback touches that need to
be protected.
